description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the archlinux:latest Docker image, defining CARGO_HOME as ${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/.cargo and PACMAN_CACHE as ${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/.pacman/pkg, and configuring a per‑job cache keyed by ${CI_JOB_NAME} that stores .cargo/, target/ and .pacman/pkg. Define three stages (build, docs, test) and a global before_script that creates the pacman cache directory, installs rust, gcc, pkg-config, make and scdoc via pacman with the custom cache, and prints rustc and cargo versions. In the build stage add a build job that runs "cargo build --release", stores the target directory as an artifact for 1 day, and has no dependencies. In the test stage add an audit job that runs "pacman -Syu --needed --noconfirm --cachedir "${PACMAN_CACHE}" cargo-audit" then "cargo audit", a check job that runs "cargo check --all --verbose --release", a clippy job that runs "find . -name '*.rs' -exec touch {} +" and "cargo clippy --all -- -D warnings", a format job that runs "cargo fmt --all -- --check", an install job that runs "install -d destdir" and "make DESTDIR=destdir PREFIX=/usr install", and a test job that runs "cargo test --all --verbose --release", each depending on the build job where indicated. In the docs stage add a completions job that runs "make completions" and publishes the target/completion/ directory as an artifact for 1 day, and a man job that runs "make docs" and publishes contrib/man/arch-audit.1 as an artifact for 1 day.
